notes: agy synchronous no-LLM slash command (/help). The whole observable is the ABSENCE of a working transition for the slash: agy slash commands are recorded in a sibling history.jsonl (verified live: /model, /permissions tagged type:slash_command), NOT in the conversation transcript.jsonl that the Source tails — and the adapter's path-shape check rejects any file but <conv-id>/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl, so history.jsonl is not watched. A no-LLM slash thus appends ZERO lines to the tailed transcript → the tailer fires no event → the session HOLDS ready across the slash window. A warm-up 'warm' real turn first materializes the transcript so the daemon binds a session (ready→working→ready); the slash_window phase pins the session ready with invariants forbidding state_transition to working/waiting; a trailing 'alive' real turn drives a SECOND ready→working→ready cycle proving the session is still alive. Single-birth adapter (transcript-first, one <conv-id> across the whole run). Tokens/cost not in the JSONL → observations assert tokens_nonzero:false, cost_nonzero:false; model omitted (provisional). INTERACTIVE driver (REPL held open across the slash window). driver=gap:wait_turn — the `slash` step IS ported (tmux send-keys to the live REPL, not a keys gap), but the warm/alive real turns need the stubbed wait_turn; record ports it + the launch_repl session-resolution SEAM first.

text: antigravity session (<conv-id>) appears in ready when transcript.jsonl is written
Warm-up 'warm' prompt → working (real turn; materializes the transcript so the daemon binds a session)
Warm-up turn completes back to ready (same <conv-id> session)

Spec: state stays ready throughout the no-LLM /help slash — it is recorded in history.jsonl, NOT the tailed transcript, so the tailer fires no event and no working/waiting transition is recorded for the slash itself
Trailing 'alive' prompt → working a SECOND time on the same <conv-id> session — proves the session is still alive after the slash window
Trailing turn completes back to ready — final state ready
